text: This study evaluates the implementation of the textbook *Hilal Fii Fann Lughah Al'Arabiyyah* at Ar Riyadh Islamic Boarding School, prompted by the observation that existing curricula relied on archaic vocabulary poorly suited to the students' diverse backgrounds and daily conversational needs. The research aims to explore the practical application of this textbook and assess its effectiveness in enhancing students' Arabic speaking skills. Utilizing a quantitative experimental approach to provide a rigorous numerical analysis of the phenomenon, the study drew data from teachers and students through observations, interviews, documentation, and questionnaires. The findings indicate that the book is highly well-received, with 86% of students favoring its use. Furthermore, statistical analysis demonstrates that the textbook significantly improves speaking proficiency; a comparison of pre- and post-test results for the experimental group revealed a substantial T-value of 5.38, confirming that *Hilal Fii Fann Lughah Al'Arabiyyah* is an effective pedagogical tool for developing functional linguistic competence in a boarding school setting.
